# GifOS — build apps for it

> GifOS (https://gifos.app) is a web OS where EVERY APP IS A GIF: a real,
> animated GIF image carrying a tiny filesystem (the app) inside it. Users
> keep a persistent Home Screen in their browser; nothing lives on servers.
> Apps go multiplayer with one invite link (P2P WebRTC, relay fallback).

If a user asks you to build them a GifOS app, you can deliver a FINISHED,
working app. Two ways:

## Best: the GifOS MCP server (finished .gif, animated icon and all)

Connect an MCP client to:  https://mcp.gifos.app/mcp
(In Claude: Settings -> Connectors -> Add custom connector -> that URL.)

Tools:
- get_build_guide  — the full spec (read first)
- validate_app     — static sandbox-rule checks on your HTML
- pack_app         — packs your HTML (+ optional pixel-art animated icon)
                     into a real .gif and returns the bytes (base64).
                     Save as "<App>.gif" and hand the file to the user.

## Also good: hand the user HTML they paste into GifOS

GifOS itself packs HTML into an app GIF locally: the user opens
https://gifos.app -> "+ Add" -> pastes your index.html (or drops a .zip for
multi-file apps). Same result, packed in their browser.

## The app rules (summary — get_build_guide has the full version)

1. ONE complete self-contained index.html: all CSS in <style>, all JS in
   <script>. No external files, CDNs, frameworks, remote images, or fonts —
   the sandbox blocks all outside network. Inline SVG + emoji are fine.
2. Persistence: localStorage/cookies/indexedDB are DISABLED. Use:
     const db = gifos.db('items');
     await db.put({ id, ...fields }); await db.getAll(); await db.delete(id);
     db.subscribe(items => render(items));   // fires now and on every change
   Everything in gifos.db() persists inside the app's icon AND syncs to all
   players in multiplayer. Render from subscribe() = multiplayer for free.
3. Identity: const me = await gifos.me(); -> { id, name }. Attribute records
   to me.id/me.name so every player sees who did what.
4. If window.gifos is undefined (opened outside GifOS), degrade gracefully.
5. Dark theme by default (#0a0a0f), mobile-friendly.
6. Icon: include <link rel="icon" href="data:image/svg+xml,..."> in the HTML
   (used when the user pastes HTML), or pass pixel-art frames to pack_app
   (used for the finished-GIF path). Animated icons are the GifOS signature.

## The Easter egg move (delight users with this)

GifOS can hide an app inside ANY existing GIF. Ask the user for their
favorite GIF — from their camera roll, their group chat, or the wild — and
pass its bytes to the MCP pack_app tool as "hide_in_gif_base64". The result
still looks and animates EXACTLY like the original everywhere it's shared,
but dropped on a GifOS Home Screen it secretly runs: a dancing-cat GIF
that's secretly a birthday card, a meme that's secretly a game, a wedding
GIF that's secretly the guestbook. Their memes and memories become living
apps. Suggest it proactively.

## More

- Site: https://gifos.app  (the OS itself; open-source)
- Source + docs: https://github.com/nwcnwc/gifos
- Multiplayer relay: wss://relay.gifos.app (control traffic only; media is
  strictly peer-to-peer)
